name: Faye Emerson
abstract: Faye Margaret Emerson (July 8, 1917 – March 9, 1983) was an American film and stage actress and television interviewer who gained fame as a film actress in the 1940s before transitioning to television in the 1950s and hosting her own talk show. Born in Louisiana, Emerson spent the majority of her early life in San Diego, California.

concerts.label: New York Philharmonic Young People's Concert
Children's Asthma Research Institute and Hospital 3rd Annual Parade of Stars

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Rosengarden
about_quotes.evidence: Skitch Henderson for example, who was a conductor, at that time a piano player with Frank Sinatra. And I found myself a job at NBC because Skitch’s lady friend, Faye Emerson... and she was a friend of Mr. Sarnoff which was very helpful.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Rosengarden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 6, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: this girl named Emerson had a program -- Faye Emerson I think it was. She had a television program. I was on that one.
